tool.name: HyPhy-B-STILL
tool.description: Bayesian Significance Test of Invariant Low Likelihoods

param.label: Grid points
param.help: The number of grid points used to approximate the posterior distribution of dN and dS.

param.name: concentration
param.label: Concentration parameter of the Dirichlet prior
param.help: The concentration parameter of the Dirichlet prior on the grid weights.

param.label: Enforce non-zero synonymous rates
param.help: Enforce non-zero synonymous rates on the grid. This is useful for calculating dN/dS ratios, as it prevents division by zero.

param.label: EBF threshold for reporting proximal invariance
param.help: Empirical Bayes Factor threshold for reporting sites under proximal constraint.

param.name: radius_threshold
param.label: Expected substitution multiplier for defining 'near-zero' selective regime
param.help: Defines the radius threshold for considering a site as proximal to invariance.

tool.help:
B-STILL : Bayesian Significance Test of Invariant Low Likelihoods ==================================================================
What question does this method answer? --------------------------------------
Which sites in a gene are effectively invariant (alpha=beta=0) or proximal to invariance, indicating sites under strong functional constraint or experiencing negligible evolutionary change?
Recommended Applications ------------------------
B-STILL is designed to identify sites that show minimal to no evolutionary change across the phylogeny. This is particularly useful for:
- Identifying functionally constrained sites that are critical for protein structure or function - Detecting sites under extreme purifying selection - Characterizing regions of genes that are evolutionarily "frozen" - Complementing positive selection analyses by identifying the most conserved sites
Brief description -----------------
B-STILL (Bayesian Significance Test of Invariant Low Likelihoods) is a modified version of FUBAR that focuses on detecting invariant sites. It uses a denser grid around zero and reports the posterior probability and Empirical Bayes Factor for sites being effectively invariant (alpha=beta=0) or proximal to invariance.
The method models non-synonymous (dN) and synonymous (dS) substitution rates at each site using a Bayesian approach with a discretized grid. Unlike standard FUBAR which focuses on detecting positive selection (dN/dS > 1), B-STILL emphasizes the detection of sites where both rates are near or at zero.
B-STILL offers three different methods for estimating the posterior distribution:
* **Variational-Bayes:** A fast approximation method that is the recommended default. * **Collapsed-Gibbs:** A faster MCMC method. * **Metropolis-Hastings:** The original, slowest MCMC method.
Input -----
1. A *FASTA* sequence alignment. 2. A phylogenetic tree in the *Newick* format
Note: the names of sequences in the alignment must match the names of the sequences in the tree.
Output ------
A JSON file with analysis results (http://hyphy.org/resources/json-fields.pdf).
A markdown report with sites identified as invariant or proximal to invariance.
Further reading ---------------
http://hyphy.org/methods/selection-methods/#FUBAR
Tool options ------------ ::
--code Which genetic code to use
--grid The number of grid points used to approximate the posterior distribution of dN and dS. A larger grid will provide a more accurate approximation but will also be slower. The default value of 20 is a good compromise between speed and accuracy.
--method The inference method to use for estimating the posterior distribution. Variational-Bayes : 0-th order Variational Bayes approximation; fastest [default] Metropolis-Hastings : Full Metropolis-Hastings MCMC algorithm; orignal method [slowest] Collapsed-Gibbs : Collapsed Gibbs sampler [intermediate speed]
--chains The number of MCMC chains to run. This is only applicable to the Metropolis-Hastings and Collapsed-Gibbs methods. A larger number of chains will provide a better exploration of the posterior distribution but will also be slower. default value: 5
--chain-length The length of each MCMC chain. This is only applicable to the Metropolis-Hastings and Collapsed-Gibbs methods. A longer chain will provide a better exploration of the posterior distribution but will also be slower. default value: 2,000,000
--burn-in The number of samples to discard from the beginning of each MCMC chain. This is done to ensure that the chain has converged to the posterior distribution. This is only applicable to the Metropolis-Hastings and Collapsed-Gibbs methods. default value: 1,000,000
--samples The number of samples to draw from each MCMC chain after the burn-in period. These samples are used to estimate the posterior distribution. This is only applicable to the Metropolis-Hastings and Collapsed-Gibbs methods. default value: 1,000
--concentration_parameter The concentration parameter of the Dirichlet prior on the grid weights. default value: 0.5
--non-zero Enforce non-zero synonymous rates on the grid. This is useful for calculating dN/dS ratios, as it prevents division by zero.
--ebf Empirical Bayes Factor threshold for reporting sites under proximal constraint. default value: 10
--radius-threshold Expected substitution multiplier for defining 'near-zero' selective regime. Defines the radius threshold for considering a site as proximal to invariance. default value: 0.5
--kill-zero-lengths Automatically delete internal zero-length branches for computational efficiency. This will not affect the results.
